# Responsive Design Reference

Use this reference when building layouts, navigation, or any viewport-dependent UI.
Responsive design is not a phase — it's part of every layout decision.

---

## Core Principles

1. **Mobile-first** — Start with the smallest viewport. Enhance for larger screens.
2. **Content drives breakpoints** — Break when the content breaks, not at device widths.
3. **Fluid over fixed** — Use relative units, flexbox, and grid. Avoid fixed pixel widths for layout.
4. **Test the in-between** — Bugs hide between breakpoints, not at them.

---

## Units

### When to Use What

| Unit | Use For |
|---|---|
| `rem` | Font sizes, spacing, padding, margins (scales with user preferences) |
| `em` | Component-local scaling (relative to parent font size) |
| `%` | Fluid widths relative to parent |
| `vw` / `vh` | Full-viewport layouts (use sparingly — beware mobile viewport issues) |
| `dvh` | Dynamic viewport height (accounts for mobile browser chrome) |
| `ch` | Text-width constraints (e.g., `max-width: 65ch` for readable line lengths) |
| `px` | Borders, shadows, fine details that should not scale |

```css
/* Base font size respects user preferences */
html {
  font-size: 100%; /* = 16px default, but scales with browser settings */
}

/* Layout uses rem */
.container {
  max-width: 75rem; /* 1200px at default font size */
  padding: 1rem;
  margin-inline: auto;
}

/* Readable text width */
.prose {
  max-width: 65ch;
}
```

---

## Mobile-First Breakpoints

```css
/* Mobile styles are the default — no media query needed */
.grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  gap: 1rem;
}

/* Tablet and up */
@media (min-width: 48rem) {
  .grid {
    grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr);
  }
}

/* Desktop and up */
@media (min-width: 80rem) {
  .grid {
    grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);
  }
}
```

Common breakpoints (in `rem` at 16px base):
| Name | rem | px equivalent |
|---|---|---|
| Small mobile | default | 0–479px |
| Mobile | 30rem | 480px |
| Tablet | 48rem | 768px |
| Desktop | 80rem | 1280px |
| Wide | 96rem | 1536px |

**Important:** These are starting points. Adjust based on when your content actually breaks.

---

## Container Queries

Use container queries when a component's layout should depend on its parent container, not the viewport.

```css
.card-container {
  container-type: inline-size;
  container-name: card;
}

.card {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}

@container card (min-width: 400px) {
  .card {
    flex-direction: row;
  }
}

@container card (min-width: 600px) {
  .card {
    grid-template-columns: 200px 1fr;
  }
}
```

Container queries are right when:
- A component is reused in sidebars, modals, and full-width layouts.
- The component needs to adapt to available space, not viewport width.

---

## Fluid Typography

Scale font sizes smoothly between breakpoints using `clamp()`.

```css
/* Fluid heading: 1.5rem at small screens, 2.5rem at large screens */
h1 {
  font-size: clamp(1.5rem, 1rem + 2vw, 2.5rem);
}

/* Fluid body text */
body {
  font-size: clamp(1rem, 0.9rem + 0.5vw, 1.125rem);
}
```

Rules:
- Never go below `1rem` (16px) for body text.
- Use `clamp()` instead of multiple media queries for font sizes.
- Test that text remains readable at both extremes.

---

## Layout Patterns

### Sidebar Layout

```css
/* Sidebar collapses below content on mobile */
.layout {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr;
  gap: 1.5rem;
}

@media (min-width: 48rem) {
  .layout {
    grid-template-columns: 16rem 1fr;
  }
}
```

### Holy Grail (Header, Sidebar, Content, Footer)

```css
.page {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-rows: auto 1fr auto;
  min-height: 100dvh;
}

.page-body {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr;
}

@media (min-width: 48rem) {
  .page-body {
    grid-template-columns: 16rem 1fr;
  }
}
```

### Card Grid (Auto-Fill)

```css
/* Cards auto-fill, no media queries needed */
.card-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(min(100%, 18rem), 1fr));
  gap: 1.5rem;
}
```

---

## Images and Media

```html
<!-- Responsive image with srcset -->
<img
  src="hero-800.jpg"
  srcset="hero-400.jpg 400w, hero-800.jpg 800w, hero-1600.jpg 1600w"
  sizes="(max-width: 48rem) 100vw, 50vw"
  alt="Product showcase"
  loading="lazy"
  decoding="async"
/>

<!-- Responsive image with art direction -->
<picture>
  <source media="(min-width: 48rem)" srcset="hero-wide.jpg" />
  <source media="(min-width: 30rem)" srcset="hero-medium.jpg" />
  <img src="hero-mobile.jpg" alt="Product showcase" />
</picture>
```

```css
/* Prevent images from overflowing */
img, video, svg {
  max-width: 100%;
  height: auto;
}

/* Aspect ratio for media containers */
.video-container {
  aspect-ratio: 16 / 9;
  width: 100%;
}
```

---

## Navigation Patterns

### Responsive Navigation

```tsx
// React
function Navigation() {
  const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = React.useState(false);

  return (
    <nav aria-label="Main">
      <button
        className="nav-toggle"
        aria-expanded={isOpen}
        aria-controls="nav-menu"
        onClick={() => setIsOpen(!isOpen)}
      >
        <span className="sr-only">{isOpen ? "Close menu" : "Open menu"}</span>
        <MenuIcon aria-hidden="true" />
      </button>

      <ul
        id="nav-menu"
        className={`nav-menu ${isOpen ? "nav-menu--open" : ""}`}
        {...(!isOpen && { inert: "" })}
      >
        <li><a href="/">Home</a></li>
        <li><a href="/products">Products</a></li>
        <li><a href="/about">About</a></li>
      </ul>
    </nav>
  );
}
```

```vue
<!-- Vue -->
<script setup lang="ts">
const isOpen = ref(false);
</script>

<template>
  <nav aria-label="Main">
    <button
      class="nav-toggle"
      :aria-expanded="isOpen"
      aria-controls="nav-menu"
      @click="isOpen = !isOpen"
    >
      <span class="sr-only">{{ isOpen ? "Close menu" : "Open menu" }}</span>
      <MenuIcon aria-hidden="true" />
    </button>

    <ul
      id="nav-menu"
      :class="['nav-menu', { 'nav-menu--open': isOpen }]"
      :inert="!isOpen || undefined"
    >
      <li><a href="/">Home</a></li>
      <li><a href="/products">Products</a></li>
      <li><a href="/about">About</a></li>
    </ul>
  </nav>
</template>
```
```

```css
.nav-toggle {
  display: block;
}

.nav-menu {
  display: none;
}

.nav-menu--open {
  display: flex;
  flex-direction: column;
}

@media (min-width: 48rem) {
  .nav-toggle {
    display: none;
  }

  .nav-menu {
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: row;
  }
}
```

---

## Touch Considerations

```css
/* Ensure touch targets are at least 44x44px */
button, a, input, select {
  min-height: 2.75rem; /* 44px */
}

/* Add spacing between touch targets */
.nav-list li + li {
  margin-top: 0.5rem;
}

/* Hover states should not be the only indicator */
@media (hover: hover) {
  .card:hover {
    box-shadow: var(--shadow-md);
  }
}

/* For touch devices, use active state instead */
@media (hover: none) {
  .card:active {
    box-shadow: var(--shadow-md);
  }
}
```

---

## Overflow and Long Content

```css
/* Prevent layout-breaking long words */
.content {
  overflow-wrap: break-word;
  word-break: break-word;
  hyphens: auto;
}

/* Truncate single-line text */
.truncate {
  overflow: hidden;
  text-overflow: ellipsis;
  white-space: nowrap;
}

/* Multi-line truncation */
.line-clamp-3 {
  display: -webkit-box;
  -webkit-line-clamp: 3;
  -webkit-box-orient: vertical;
  overflow: hidden;
}

/* Tables that don't overflow */
.table-container {
  overflow-x: auto;
  -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch;
}
```

---

## Testing Checklist

- [ ] Layout works at 320px, 768px, and 1280px+ viewports
- [ ] No horizontal scrolling on any viewport
- [ ] Content reflows correctly at 200% browser zoom
- [ ] Touch targets are at least 44x44px
- [ ] Text is readable without zooming (16px+ base)
- [ ] Long text and dynamic content don't break layout
- [ ] Images scale correctly, no overflow
- [ ] Navigation is usable on all screen sizes
- [ ] Hover-dependent interactions have touch/keyboard alternatives
- [ ] Tested on actual devices, not just browser resize (if possible)
